edges.description: Weak type III collagen matrix reduces tensile strength in arteries, bowel, uterus, and other organs, producing spontaneous or trauma-triggered dissection, rupture, and perforation.
Defective type III collagen matrix contributes to thin translucent skin.
Perivascular connective-tissue fragility increases bruising susceptibility.
Dermal connective-tissue fragility produces acrogeric, prematurely aged facial and skin features.
Arterial wall fragility can culminate in spontaneous arterial rupture.
Fragile arterial walls can separate along vessel-wall layers before or without rupture.
Hollow-organ fragility can produce gastrointestinal perforation.
Gravid uterine connective-tissue fragility can result in pregnancy-associated uterine rupture.

nodes.description: Weak type III collagen matrix in arteries, bowel, uterus, and other organs produces spontaneous or minor-trauma vascular rupture, dissection, gastrointestinal perforation, and pregnancy-associated uterine rupture.

evidence.snippet: Vascular dissection or rupture, gastrointestinal perforation, or organ rupture are the presenting signs in most adults with vEDS.

vascular EDS (vEDS, OMIM#130050) is the most severe type and is primarily characterized by life-threatening features of tissue fragility leading to arterial dissection or aneurysm, gastrointestinal ruptures, and pregnancy complications at a young age

nodes.description: Arterial dissection is a major vascular fragility manifestation in vEDS.

evidence.snippet: Arterial rupture may be preceded by aneurysm, arteriovenous fistulae, or dissection but also may occur spontaneously.

nodes.description: Arterial rupture or dissection is a hallmark and life-threatening vEDS manifestation.

evidence.snippet: Arterial rupture may be preceded by aneurysm, arteriovenous fistulae, or dissection but also may occur spontaneously.

nodes.description: Easy bruising reflects perivascular connective-tissue fragility.

evidence.snippet: characterized by arterial, intestinal, and/or uterine fragility; thin, translucent skin; easy bruising

nodes.description: Gastrointestinal perforation is part of the hollow-organ fragility phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Vascular dissection or rupture, gastrointestinal perforation, or organ rupture are the presenting signs in most adults with vEDS.

nodes.description: Acrogeric, prematurely aged appearance is a frequent vEDS connective-tissue feature.

evidence.snippet: HP:0007495 | Prematurely aged appearance | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Thin translucent skin is a characteristic connective-tissue feature.

evidence.snippet: characterized by arterial, intestinal, and/or uterine fragility; thin, translucent skin; easy bruising

nodes.description: COL3A1 variants disrupt type III collagen triple-helix formation, secretion, and extracellular-matrix deposition. The resulting collagen III matrix weakness compromises blood-vessel and hollow-organ tensile strength, leading to arterial dissection or rupture, intestinal perforation, uterine rupture, easy bruising, and thin translucent skin.

evidence.snippet: vEDS is caused by mutations in COL3A1 encoding collagen III that shows a predominant expression in blood vessels and hollow organs
Most disease-causing variants in COL3A1 are glycine substitutions that destroy the triple helical winding

nodes.description: Uterine rupture during pregnancy is a life-threatening hollow-organ complication in affected women.

evidence.snippet: Treatment may include medical or surgical management for arterial complications, bowel rupture, or uterine rupture during pregnancy.
